name: edit_post
description:
Edit an existing post in Buffer. Before calling edit_post, call get_post with the target post ID to retrieve the current schedulingType, shareMode, dueAt, text, assets, metadata, and allowedActions. Verify that updatePost is in allowedActions before proceeding. Pass schedulingType and mode unchanged from get_post unless the user explicitly requests rescheduling or a mode change. Both schedulingType and mode are required on every call.

Every edit is re-validated as a whole post against the same rules as creation; it is not merged with the stored post. Carry the current assets and metadata forward from get_post and change only what the user asked to change. Dropping an asset or metadata field the post requires will reject the edit.

Asset behavior: omitting the assets field preserves stored media, but a post that requires media must still include it, so carrying assets forward is the safe default. Pass an empty array to clear all assets. Pass a non-empty array to replace them.

Returns the updated post with id, status, and scheduling details.

postId.description: The ID of the post to edit. Use list_posts or get_post to find post IDs.

schedulingType.description: Publishing method: notification (manual approval) or automatic (auto-publish). Pass the value returned by get_post unless the user explicitly wants to change it.

mode.description: How to schedule: addToQueue (add to queue), shareNow (publish immediately), shareNext (publish at next scheduled slot), customScheduled (requires dueAt with an ISO 8601 datetime). Pass the value returned by get_post (as `shareMode`) unless the user explicitly requests rescheduling.

dueAt.description: Scheduled time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (e.g., "2025-03-15T17:00:00-05:00"). Required for customScheduled mode. Use the timezone from get_account to construct the correct offset for the user's local time. Anchor relative times ("tomorrow", "in 2 hours") on currentTime from get_account; dueAt must be after currentTime, never in the past.

assets.description: Media attachments. To carry existing media forward from get_post, map each asset: use source as url and, for image assets only, thumbnail as thumbnailUrl. Never set thumbnailUrl on video assets - it is not supported; use the video metadata.thumbnailOffset to pick a thumbnail frame instead (Instagram, TikTok, and Pinterest only). Omit this field to preserve stored media; pass an empty array to clear all assets; pass a non-empty array to replace them.

url.description: Direct URL to the image file.

x.description: The x coordinate of the user tag (0-1).

y.description: The y coordinate of the user tag (0-1).

url.description: Direct URL to the video file.
Direct URL to the document file.
Link URL.

metadata.description: Service-specific options. Carry the existing metadata forward from get_post unless the user is changing it; dropping a required metadata field (such as an Instagram post's type) will reject the edit.

shouldShareToFeed.description: Share reel to feed (required, use true for reels).

text.description: Text for the Story or Reel.

url.description: URL the annotation points to.
Link URL (required).
Link URL (required).

thread.description: Thread items array. IMPORTANT: For threaded posts, you must provide BOTH the outer text field AND this thread array. The outer text should match the first thread item's text. Include ALL thread items here.

text.description: Text content for this thread item.

assets.description: Media assets for this thread item.
